judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports nail the default-on behavior, the fix, and the copy-paste provenance from compact_calendar_view_enabled, but only Report 1 identifies the missing environment/deploy-level master gate and recommends adding one, so Report 1 answered the task better overall.

report_1.evidence: Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant)` in `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... when `"auto_send_reminders"` is absent from `tenant.settings` (or explicitly `null`), `.get()` returns `None`, and the function returns `True`.

report_2.evidence: Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... if the tenant's JSONB `settings` dict has no `"auto_send_reminders"` key (i.e., the clinic admin has never touched the toggle), `.get()` returns `None`, and the function returns `True`.

report_1.evidence: I would change `auto_send_reminders_enabled` (`slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`) so that `None`/missing returns `False` instead of `True`... This makes reminders strictly opt-in

report_2.evidence: I would change the missing-value branch in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` (`backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`) to return `False` instead of `True`, so the feature is opt-in per clinic

report_1.evidence: `git show aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214, 2026-07-01) then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` immediately below it in the same file... with the byte-for-byte identical `is None → return True` / `bool(...)` structure... `compact_calendar_view_enabled` at `feature_flags.py:4-7`, which gates a UI display preference — worst case of a wrong default there is a mildly annoying calendar layout, not a message a patient didn't expect to receive.

report_2.evidence: Commit `aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214) then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` immediately below it in the same file, using the identical `is None: return True` structure... The default was inherited by pattern-matching against a neighboring flag rather than by reasoning about this feature's specific consequences. `compact_calendar_view` defaulting to "on" for missing settings is low-stakes (internal UI cosmetics...); `auto_send_reminders` defaulting to "on" governs whether third-party patients receive unsolicited communications

report_1.evidence: **Confirm there's no env-level master switch.** project documentation's recipe says a per-tenant flag should be ANDed with an env-level master gate. I found no such master switch anywhere in this repo (`infra/main.tf` only defines an `environment` variable) — decide whether one is needed before flipping any tenant to "on" in production, so there's a single kill switch independent of per-tenant data.
